name: Pixal3D
description: Pixal3D generates high-fidelity textured 3D assets from a single image.

overview.body:
Install sets up the local Pixal3D runtime. The install options below are for building FlashAttention during setup. Auto-detect is recommended. If the build runs out of memory, retry with lower Max jobs or NVCC threads.

Fetch Models downloads the Pixal3D checkpoints and helper models. Before Fetch Models, fill the BRIA RMBG-2.0 form with the same Hugging Face account used for your token.

options.label: Auto-detect
options.description: Read the NVIDIA compute capability and select the flash-attn target arch

options.label: SM80 / RTX 30+40
options.description: 8.x GPUs: RTX 30/40, A100/A30/A10/A40, L4/L40, RTX A/Ada

options.label: SM90 / H100/H200
options.description: Hopper GPUs: GH200, H200, H100, H800

options.label: SM100 / B200
options.description: Datacenter Blackwell: GB200, B200, GB300, B300

options.label: SM110 / Jetson Thor
options.description: Jetson Thor targets: T5000 and T4000

options.label: SM120 / RTX 50
options.description: RTX 50-series and RTX PRO Blackwell workstation/server GPUs

options.label: MAX_JOBS=2
options.description: Lowest RAM pressure for the flash-attn source build

options.label: MAX_JOBS=4
options.description: Recommended flash-attn build cap for 32GB RAM

options.label: MAX_JOBS=8
options.description: More CPU and RAM use during the flash-attn source build

options.label: NVCC_THREADS=1
options.description: Safest compiler thread setting for lower RAM pressure

options.label: NVCC_THREADS=2
options.description: Moderate compiler threading if the machine has RAM headroom

options.label: NVCC_THREADS=4
options.description: Aggressive compiler threading; can multiply memory pressure with Max jobs

detail_primary_action.label: Fill BRIA form
detail_primary_action.heading: NEXT STEP:
detail_primary_action.help: BRIA form needs filling before Fetch Models.

options.label: Low VRAM 1024
options.description: Recommended first test; lower peak VRAM and 1024 cascade

options.label: Standard 1536
options.description: Heavier profile for high VRAM systems

notes: The official upstream training code and data toolkit are preserved in the module repo, but v0.1 exposes inference and Gradio testing only.
Install/Repair installs the official Pixal3D utils3d wheel, verifies intrinsics_from_fov_xy, and creates the utils3d.pt/np compatibility aliases required by MoGe camera estimation.
The upstream custom UI is kept only as reference code; Manager opens the NymphsCore Pixal3D UI.
Closing the embedded Pixal3D UI runs the full kill action so the API/UI backend does not stay alive after the WebView closes.
Pixal3D keeps stop and kill as installed entrypoints for lifecycle cleanup, while the visible emergency Kill switch lives inside the Pixal3D UI.
Open UI starts the local Gradio server/API and opens the NymphsCore custom UI.
The Gradio launcher no longer prints the API URL during API pre-start, and status reports the Gradio URL while the Gradio UI is running.
The API running check now also accepts a healthy /server_info probe so the Gradio launcher does not spawn a duplicate API on an occupied port.
Low-VRAM Gradio exposes Texture NAF Size and defaults the texture conditioning NAF pass to 512 to reduce 16 GB GPU pressure.
Gradio is allowed to serve generated GLB files from the shared Pixal3D outputs directory.
v0.1.27 republishes the dual UI manifest so installed 0.1.26 runtimes surface a normal Manager update.
v0.1.28 makes Nymphs Ui the primary Manager UI.
v0.1.29 keeps the Gradio start output free of the base server URL so Manager opens the explicit Pixal3D UI URL.
v0.1.30 exposes all current generation/export parameters in Nymphs Ui and keeps the background-removal model on GPU by default to avoid low-VRAM CPU unload failures.
v0.1.31 compacts Nymphs Ui for the default Manager WebView size and removes the duplicate main reference preview.
v0.1.32 adds detailed Nymphs Ui progress text under the run bar and reports model-loading, preprocess, camera, sampling, rendering, and export stages.
v0.1.33 copies the official source-prep flow in Nymphs Ui: image selection now shows preprocessing progress and enables Generate only after the source is ready.
v0.1.34 sharpens and compacts Nymphs Ui typography, disables hidden delayed warmup by default, and prevents warmup polling from fighting source-prep progress.
v0.1.35 mirrors run progress into the top bar with compact stage text and a slim progress bar.
v0.1.36 moves RMBG source prep to CPU by default and caps Pixal3D CUDA allocator use to reduce device-not-ready failures on 16 GB GPUs.
v0.1.37 keeps Pixal3D texture NAF target aligned with the 1024 default instead of silently lowering it separately from the visible GLB texture size.

v0.1.38 keeps the Nymphs Ui on the official-style source-prep flow, removes the separate NAF helper size control, and keeps texture NAF aligned with the visible texture path while retaining the low-VRAM CUDA/RMBG protections.
v0.1.39 moves the mirrored run progress into a dedicated compact row directly under the result topbar so it remains visible at the default Manager WebView size.

v0.1.40 keeps Nymphs Ui on the official preprocess-then-generate flow, removes the left progress card, and moves the activity icon, stage, detail text, and progress bar into the top-right result strip.

v0.1.41 adds a Prepare source image toggle. It defaults on for the official preprocess-then-generate path, while advanced users can turn it off to generate from the original source image without hidden preprocessing.
v0.1.42 passes the server-side preprocessed file path into Generate instead of its localhost URL, avoiding Gradio safehttpx localhost validation failures.
v0.1.43 installs the official Pixal3D utils3d wheel, verifies intrinsics_from_fov_xy, and creates utils3d.pt/np aliases for MoGe instead of installing the incompatible newer MoGe utils3d commit.
v0.1.44 expands the Nymphs Ui top/result progress bar to the full result strip width.
v0.1.45 stops the Pixal3D UI during module update and detects a running Gradio URL even when the pid file is stale, preventing fixed dependencies from being hidden by stale Python imports.
v0.1.46 refreshes README and handoff docs with the utils3d and stale-Gradio-process lessons from test WSL validation.
v0.1.47 keeps delayed model warmup progress from overwriting the selected-image ready state in Nymphs Ui.
v0.1.48 removes duplicate in-app result/title labels and moves delayed model warmup into a tiny separate topbar indicator.
v0.1.49 styles Nymphs Ui scrollbars as thin teal thumbs with transparent tracks to match the rest of the app.
v0.1.50 uses compact Weight Profile display labels in Nymphs Ui so the two-column parameter controls stay readable.
v0.1.51 moves the main generation progress strip below the preview and repairs missing nvdiffrec_render during install/update.
v0.1.52 treats nvdiffrec_render as a shared TRELLIS.2/Pixal3D runtime dependency and installs it after nvdiffrast with CUDA arch scoping.
v0.1.53 makes Pixal3D uninstall output and metadata explicit that the shared TRELLIS.2/Pixal3D runtime venv is preserved.

v0.1.54 makes the NymphsCore UI the only advertised Pixal3D app surface, adds optimized run profiles, exposes max tokens and texture NAF controls, adds manual Free Pipeline, removes the default CUDA memory fraction cap, and exports GLB textures as PNG.
v0.1.55 adds the WSL CUDA driver library path while building nvdiffrec_render so the Pixal3D-specific renderer can link against libcuda.so.
v0.1.56 refreshes module files on every install/repair so a retry after a failed partial install cannot leave stale UI, docs, or manifest files behind.
v0.1.57 serves the NymphsCore UI directly through FastAPI endpoints so Pixal3D no longer depends on the removed Gradio Server/app.api path in Gradio 6.
v0.1.58 widens the Run Profile selector, places Resolution next to Low VRAM, renames Free Pipeline to Clear GPU Memory, and prevents stale source-prep progress from lingering after prep is disabled.
v0.1.59 adds a visible Stop module action for the running Pixal3D UI/API process.
v0.1.60 adds a manual Warm Up control to the NymphsCore UI and a backend warmup endpoint while keeping optional warm-on-open support.

v0.1.61 gives Warm Up its own top progress bar, gates source prep and Generate until warmup is ready, widens the locked control column, enlarges the source preview, compacts buttons, and renames runtime presets with technical settings.

v0.1.62 auto-starts warmup when the NymphsCore UI opens, keeps source prep waiting for warmup, unlocks Generate only after prep is ready, adds Clear Source and Retry Prep controls, tunes the default Pixal3D split, keeps action buttons compact, and surfaces source-prep backend errors in the UI.

v0.1.63 makes source prep manual with a Prepare Source/Retry Prep button, keeps the warmup strip warmup-only, fixes runtime preset Low VRAM sync, and changes the source preview to a portrait-friendly shape.
v0.1.64 restyles Pixal3D NymphsCore UI actions as compact app-style // command buttons and fixes the source preview image containment so selected images do not clip inside the preview box.

v0.1.75 hardens Nymphs Ui warmup/run state, prevents Clear GPU Memory and source replacement during active tasks, preserves runtime settings through GLB export, vendors viewer scripts locally, and surfaces generation/export errors in the UI.

v0.1.76 exposes deeper generation startup progress in the existing progress strip, shortens the ready warmup state to Warmed, preserves the current GLB when the same source image is touched again, makes Warm Up restart/reconnect the backend when applying changed runtime memory settings, and makes Clear GPU Memory reset the backend so settings can be changed before warming.
v0.1.77 moves Open GLB and Clear GPU Memory into the top command strip, defaults Use GPU for RMBG on, and stops all Pixal3D backends when the Nymphs UI/WebView unloads.
v0.1.78 groups the top Pixal3D commands into a tighter run cluster and a lower-priority output/memory utility cluster.

v0.1.79 forces Use GPU for RMBG on when the Nymphs UI loads, makes preprocessing default to GPU RMBG if no value is supplied, and aligns the command buttons into neat full-width source, run, and utility rows.
v0.1.80 adds an always-present Manager Kill action that runs the hard Pixal3D stop script outside the blocked web backend.
v0.1.81 moves the user-facing Kill switch into the Pixal3D UI, removes visible outer Kill/Stop module buttons, makes UI close run kill, and compacts the in-UI command rows.
v0.1.82 clears stale warmup/progress state after in-UI Kill and lets Warm restart the backend through the module action bridge before warming.
v0.1.83 keeps the killed-state recovery inside the Pixal3D UI: Warm starts Pixal3D again, reconnects, and warms without telling users to reopen the UI.
v0.1.84 runs blocking Pixal3D UI API tasks on worker threads so /progress can report backend stages during generation and GLB export.
v0.1.85 adds conservative per-run CUDA cleanup and memory breadcrumbs after generation, camera estimation, and GLB export to reduce second-run OOM risk.

v0.1.86 makes BRIA RMBG-2.0/Hugging Face access requirements explicit in install-facing copy and Fetch Models text, including that BRIA is downloaded during Fetch Models and requires the same HF account to accept access first.
v0.1.87 replaces the long Pixal3D details text with concise user-facing setup guidance and makes the BRIA form the explicit next step before Fetch Models.
v0.1.88 adds a module-owned installed NEXT STEP action that opens the BRIA form before Fetch Models.
v0.1.89 moves the BRIA form next step into the Manager Details-pane primary action contract.
v0.1.90 keeps Pixal3D details copy minimal and shows the BRIA form action while model download or status checking is needed.
v0.1.91 makes the pre-install BRIA form requirement clearer on the first details page.
v0.1.92 removes GGUF choices from the normal Fetch Models UI/status for now and emits a clear BRIA next-step failure when RMBG access is missing.

v0.1.93 fixes Fetch Models argument handling and status reporting so Low VRAM 1024 and Standard 1536 show as cached when the shared Pixal3D safetensors cache is present, even while BRIA RMBG remains missing.
v0.1.94 tightens the available-state Details copy so the Pixal3D description and BRIA setup step are clear without repeating.
v0.1.95 keeps the Model Fetch controls available after models are cached; only the temporary BRIA next-step prompt is state-gated.
v0.1.96 restores the plain install-options title and makes fresh installs self-heal missing Python 3.10 venv/dev apt packages before creating the shared runtime.
v0.1.97 adds a short Details-pane note explaining that the install options control the FlashAttention build.
v0.1.98 fixes standalone shared-venv install order so utils3d is validated before nvdiffrast is required, while final runtime validation still checks the full Pixal3D stack.
v0.1.99 makes standalone Pixal3D install self-heal a missing CUDA 13 toolkit/nvcc before building FlashAttention.
v0.1.100 skips duplicate latent decoding in the Manager generation path, clears per-run latent caches, and adds opt-in backend auto-free controls for repeat-run OOM diagnostics.
v0.1.101 resets the Manager model-viewer WebGL scene before new generation, source replacement, and GLB loading so stale preview meshes do not stay resident across CUDA runs.

v0.1.102 moves the Manager custom UI onto isolated single-use Pixal3D CUDA workers for source prep and combined generate/export, keeping FlashAttention enabled while avoiding second generation reuse of a spent CUDA process.

v0.1.103 restores the stable 0.1.101 Manager runtime flow after the isolated-worker experiment caused confusing double pipeline loads and hangs; repeat-run diagnostics remain source-only for root-cause investigation.
v0.1.104 offloads the BRIA RMBG source-prep model after preprocessing in low-VRAM mode so repeated Manager runs start generation with clean CUDA memory while keeping FlashAttention enabled.
v0.1.105 stops retaining unused per-step sampler prediction tensors during inference, reducing peak memory pressure at the shape SLat step where repeat runs could crash WSL.
v0.1.106 trims the native CPU heap during cleanup and logs process RSS, targeting second-run WSL crashes where CUDA memory is clean but the warmed process remains near the WSL RAM ceiling.
v0.1.107 cache-busts Manager UI prepared-image and GLB preview URLs and clears stale result state even when the same source file is selected again.
v0.1.108 prevents shape decode from collapsing to an empty sparse tensor by preserving one fallback child voxel per active parent when inferred subdivision masks are all false.

v0.1.109 unifies Pixal3D runtime startup around the Manager/Nymph UI app server on port 8097 so Manager and Blender use the same warm-up, preprocess, generate, export, and cleanup flow instead of the older separate 8096 API wrapper.
v0.1.110 aligns CUDA environment setup with the Base Runtime CUDA 13 WSL targets/x86_64-linux include and library layout.
v0.1.111 stops installing CUDA from the module installer; Pixal3D now requires Base Runtime CUDA to be present and tells users to repair Base Runtime if nvcc is missing.
Do not vendor model weights into this repo.
